channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israeli Minister Smotrich Claims ICC Prosecutor Seeking Secret Arrest Warrant
item.description: Far-right Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich said the ICC prosecutor requested a secret arrest warrant against him, calling it "a declaration of war." He retaliated by ordering the demolition of the Palestinian Bedouin village of Khan al-Ahmar.

item.title: 🇱🇧 At Least 22 Killed in Israeli Attacks on Lebanon Despite Ceasefire Extension
item.description: Lebanon Health Ministry reports 22 deaths in 24 hours from Israeli strikes, bringing the total death toll since March 2 to 3,042. Israel issued forced displacement orders for 12 towns in southern Lebanon.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia sidelines Lavrov from Ukraine peace talks
item.description: Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov has been cut out of Moscow's Ukraine negotiations, with officials saying he has 'no say' in peace talks. Other Kremlin figures like Yuri Ushakov now drive Russia's outreach to Washington.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK quietly issues waivers for Russian oil imports
item.description: The UK issued licenses allowing imports of diesel and jet fuel made from Russian oil if processed in third countries, plus a separate license for maritime transport of Russian LNG. The waivers come amid rising fuel costs from the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese carrier Liaoning begins western Pacific drills
item.description: China's Liaoning aircraft carrier group launched live-fire exercises in the western Pacific, months after a stand-off with Japanese fighter jets during December training. The drills come amid tense Beijing-Tokyo relations.

item.title: ⚔️ MQ-25A Stingray cleared for deployment
item.description: The US Navy's first carrier-based unmanned aircraft reached milestone C, moving into production and deployment. The Boeing Stingray will relieve F/A-18 Super Hornets from aerial refueling duties, extending carrier strike group reach.

item.title: 🦠 WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo a Global Health Emergency
item.description: A rare Bundibugyo strain of Ebola has killed at least 131 people in eastern Congo, prompting the WHO to declare a public health emergency of international concern as cases spread to Uganda.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Europe Considers Secret Military Alliance to Replace NATO Dependence
item.description: The Economist reports European officials are quietly developing contingency plans for collective defense without US involvement, amid growing concerns about American commitment to the alliance.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Warns of More Strikes on Iran, Says Tehran Wants Deal
item.description: President Trump said he was an hour away from ordering another attack on Iran before postponing it. Tehran denies ongoing talks but Trump claims diplomatic progress.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇱🇻 Russia Warns Latvia Over Ukraine Drone Launches, Threatens Retaliation
item.description: Moscow warned that NATO membership would not protect Baltic states if Ukraine launches drones from their territory. The threat follows a NATO jet shooting down a Ukrainian drone over Estonia.

item.title: 🌍💰 G7 Finance Ministers Call for Action on Global Trade Imbalances
item.description: Finance ministers from G7 nations agreed on the need to address trade imbalances in a fragmented global economy, though concrete measures remain unspecified amid Iran war fallout.

item.title: 🇪🇺🛡️ NATO Commander Downplays US Troop Withdrawals from Europe
item.description: General Alexus Grynkewich said no more than 5,000 US troops would be withdrawn in the near term, reassuring allies after Trump announced partial drawdowns.

item.title: 🇨🇺 Cuba Warns of "Bloodbath" if US Attacks as Washington Expands Sanctions
item.description: President Díaz-Canel stressed Cuba's right to defend itself after reports Havana obtained over 300 military drones from an unspecified source. Washington expanded sanctions amid rising tensions.

item.title: 🇨🇩 Ebola Death Toll in DR Congo Rises to at Least 131
item.description: The estimated death toll from an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has risen sharply. US Secretary of State Rubio criticized the WHO for being "late" in identifying the outbreak.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Endorses Paxton in Texas Senate Race, Snubbing Cornyn
item.description: President Trump endorsed Attorney General Ken Paxton in Texas' Senate GOP primary runoff, delivering a blow to incumbent Senator John Cornyn ahead of next week's election.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canadas inflation jumps to 2.8% as Iran war drives gasoline prices higher
item.description: Annual inflation rate accelerated in April as oil price surge from Middle East conflict pushed gasoline costs up.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Explosions on Irans Qeshm Island from enemy ammunition disposal
item.description: Iranian officials report explosions on Qeshm Island were caused by neutralizing unexploded enemy ammunition, amid ongoing tensions in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Four US LNG vessels sailing to China after Trump-Xi summit
item.description: First direct US LNG cargoes to China during Trump second term signal potential energy cooperation despite geopolitical tensions.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japans strong Q1 GDP faces Iran war reality check
item.description: Japans economy grew faster than expected in Q1, but energy shock from Iran conflict threatens momentum.

item.title: 🇪🇪 NATO Fighter Jet Shoots Down Ukrainian Drone Over Estonia
item.description: A NATO fighter jet intercepted and shot down a Ukrainian drone that had entered Estonian airspace, highlighting the complexity of airspace management as Ukraine defends against Russian attacks.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE Says Drones That Hit Near Nuclear Plant Were Launched From Iraq
item.description: The UAE confirmed six drones were launched against it from Iraq in the past 48 hours, including one that caused a fire at the Barakah nuclear power plant, amid escalating regional tensions.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Russians Covertly Trained by China Return to Fight in Ukraine, Intelligence Sources Say
item.description: About 200 Russian military personnel were secretly trained by Chinese armed forces late last year and have returned to fight in Ukraine, according to European intelligence agencies.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Cautiously Optimistic on US Arms Sales Despite Trump Uncertainty
item.description: Taiwan Defense Minister Wellington Koo expressed cautious optimism about US arms sales as President Trump considers whether to proceed with new weapons deals to the self-governing island.

item.title: 🛡️ Pentagon awards M contract for counter-drone tech amid Iran war demand surge
item.description: The Defense Department awarded Perennial Autonomy a M contract to accelerate procurement of AI-enabled counter-drone systems including Merops interceptors and Bumblebee quadcopters, as the Iran conflict accelerates demand for defensive drone technology.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian strikes damage 25 homes in Kharkiv, hit Izmail port infrastructure
item.description: Russia launched overnight strikes across Ukraine, injuring three people and damaging 25 homes in Kharkiv while hitting port infrastructure in Odesa's Izmail district. Rescue teams pulled two people from rubble as search operations continued.
